title: costularia yarrabensis
intro: Costularia yarrabensis is a species of flowering plant in the sedge family, Cyperaceae, native to Australia. The medicinal properties of Costularia yarrabensis include its use in traditional Aboriginal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its health benefits are attributed to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and ground into a powder, or made into a tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, allowing its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic use.
